title: willughbeia racemosa
intro: Willughbeia racemosa, also known as the "Willughbeia tree", is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. It is native to the Pacific island nations of Vanuatu and Fiji. The medicinal properties of Willughbeia racemosa lie in its alkaloid compounds, which have been found to exhibit antimicrobial and antimalarial properties. The plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, and is often prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures for consumption.
